subject: Johnny Noviello
summary: 49-year-old Canadian citizen and U.S. lawful permanent resident (34 years) died Jun 23, 2025 at Miami Federal Detention Center. Had epilepsy requiring daily medication - facility didn't stock Klonopin, only gave 75% of Tegretol dosage. Father delivered medication, never confirmed if Johnny received it (returned after death). Day 6: 'had not eaten in a while'. Day 25: reported depression, refused medical eval, mental health referral requested - NO FOLLOW-UP. Day 39: found unresponsive, hypothermia (95.5°F), severe hypoglycemia (less than half normal). ICE report: 'had been deteriorating for a while, probably not eating enough.' Family couldn't reach him (1 call in 5 weeks). 39 days in custody. Cause of death 'under investigation' 7+ months later. ENTIRELY PREVENTABLE.

key_findings.facility: Bureau of Prisons Federal Detention Center, Miami, Florida
critical_failures: Epilepsy medication crisis: facility didn't stock Klonopin, only 75% Tegretol dosage
Father's medication delivery never confirmed received
Day 6: 'had not eaten in a while' - malnutrition ignored for 33 days
Day 25: depression, refused medical care, mental health referral requested - NO FOLLOW-UP
Prolonged deterioration: hypothermia (95.5°F), severe hypoglycemia at death
ICE admission: 'had been deteriorating for a while, probably not eating enough'
Family communication blocked (1 call in 5 weeks)
key_findings.pattern: Medical neglect, inadequate medication, malnutrition ignored, mental health crisis ignored, prolonged deterioration, family blocked
key_findings.ice_admission: ICE death report: 'Findings suggesting that Mr. Noviello had been deteriorating for a while and among other things was probably not eating enough'
key_findings.death_circumstances: Found unresponsive at 12:54 PM, no pulse, hypothermia, severe hypoglycemia. CPR and AED administered. Pronounced dead at scene by Miami Fire Rescue at 1:36 PM.
key_findings.family_advocacy: Father Angelo Noviello, 80, delivered epilepsy medication to facility - never confirmed if received. Family 'painstakingly' worked to ensure meds. 'We are destroyed.' Could not reach Johnny except 1 call in 5 weeks.
key_findings.investigation_status: Cause of death under investigation 7+ months after death. Medical Examiner report not released. ICE death report published (90-day requirement) but lists no cause.
key_findings.medication_crisis: Facility did not stock required Klonopin. Only provided 75% of required Tegretol dosage. Family medication delivery blocked/never confirmed. Medication returned with belongings after death.
